title: Integrated Intermediate Modeling Capstone · Mixed Practice Return
summary: Build a decision model from messy requirements, data, constraints, and alternatives. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a stakeholder-ready computational modeling portfolio after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Decompose an unfamiliar decision into linked algebraic, statistical, and computational submodels.
Compare multiple plausible models with validation evidence and complexity costs.
Propagate uncertainty through best, expected, and adverse scenarios.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Problem Decomposition evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Problem Decomposition assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Decompose an unfamiliar decision into linked algebraic, statistical, and computational submodels. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.text: Compare plausible approaches against the decision, constraints, evidence quality, and cost of being wrong.

stimulus.title: Model Governance case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: math intermediate integrated capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A deployed model needs no ownership or maintenance plan.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Document data provenance, revisions, approvals, and conditions requiring model retirement. Artifact under review: a stakeholder-ready computational modeling portfolio.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Model Governance, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Model Governance applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Document data provenance, revisions, approvals, and conditions requiring model retirement.
